evidence_quotes.quote: the online player platforms itch.io and Steam started censoring content that had been added and purchased.
video games as an art form also disguise social issues that may be uncomfortable for many.
Now only pornographic toys have been deleted, but what will be the next?

paragraphs.text: Dear decision-makers, The events that have taken place over the past few weeks have led me to feel serious concerns. The whole started with the abuse of power by the world’s leading payment processing companies. As a result, the online player platforms itch.io and Steam started censoring content that had been added and purchased. It is true that there is a lot of content on these platforms that is not for human consumption, but it is not for payment processing companies to take decisions by external pressure. Even if a covert fascist group (Collective SHOUT) puts pressure on these companies. These firms have no decision-making power and cannot force their clients to censor their own business. This is a dangerous terrain, in particular that video games as an art form also disguise social issues that may be uncomfortable for many. Now only pornographic toys have been deleted, but what will be the next? Delete games about gender minorities? Banning stories of migratory difficulties? The only problem with censorship is that it never stops when it achieves its original goals. It is clearly a problem that it is easy to find problematic content on the internet, but this is not a reason to censor everything and make access subject to full control. As unfair practice, we should not talk about the inaccessibility of video games. I hope that the Stop Killing Games under my control will achieve its goal.
